resources.string: Shows wheel connection status and telemetry
You long-pressed %1$s. Delete just this log, or delete all. To share it instead, tap Cancel and then tap the file.
Delete every saved crash log? This cannot be undone.
Trip will be backed up shortly
Start a recording or import trips. This shortcut opens the most recent one.
Volume is low, voice might not be audible
To find your wheel, the app needs Bluetooth and location access
Permission was denied. Open system settings to allow Bluetooth and location access
Trip recording saves GPS points so you can see your route on a map. Grant location access to continue
Preliminary supported wheel. Tap to report issues.

Support for this wheel is preliminary, only the InMotion V14 / V12 / P6 family is verified by the author. KingSong, Veteran, Begode, Ninebot and the older InMotion models ship as Preview and need rider testing. Use it with caution.\n\nTapping this banner will report an issue, so the author can fix what doesn\'t work.
Bypass BLE, feed canned responses. Debug builds only.
Keep the background service running, or stop everything?
Slow down to lock the wheel
Long-press to drag, tap to edit
Long-press and drag onto a slot above to use
Invalid hex, use byte pairs like 4c 6b 41 70
Clears the rolling history buffer for every metric. Settings and trip records are not affected.
Speed margin remaining before tilt-back kicks in. Big number = more safety buffer.
Peak speed during the current trip (resets on a new trip).
Trip distance divided by riding time so far.
Total watt-hours drawn from the battery this trip.
Distance remaining at the current consumption rate.
Energy used per kilometre, lower is more efficient.
Road grade calculated from the wheel\'s pitch, positive is uphill.
Estimated from wheel-speed change (not the IMU), works regardless of phone mounting.
Total watt-hours fed back to the battery while braking this trip.
Bluetooth signal strength to the wheel, closer to 0 dBm is stronger.
GPS positioning error radius, lower is more accurate.
Install the EUC Planet companion app on a Wear OS or Garmin watch to start receiving the dial here
Phone hotspot is off. Make sure the HUD and the phone are on the same WiFi network.
Phone hotspot is broadcasting. Connect the HUD to this phone\'s WiFi to enable the link.
Import an Overlay Studio preset onto the HUD\'s Custom screen, without backgrounds. The preset is copied here, so later edits in the studio won\'t change the HUD until you import again
HUD heads-up display companion server stream telemetry hotspot motoeye turn arrow
Double-tap a HUD button to run an action. A single tap still switches screens
Need the HUD app? Get it at eucplanet.ried.no/hud
Keep the watch awake while the app is open
Slower updates save battery on the phone and watch
Open the watch app together with the phone app
Stop the watch app on Stop all
Show the unit label next to the big speed number
Make the PWM bar and number larger than the speed
Holds may be intercepted by the system on some watches
Depends on what your watch supports
Garmin: Start · Galaxy Watch Ultra: orange Action button
Garmin: Up (long press) · Galaxy Watch Ultra: lower side button
Brief watch vibration on each press, where supported
Your backup folder for trips, settings, themes and overlays
New trips are saved to your folder automatically
%1$d trips exist on both Dropbox and this phone.
Your current settings will be replaced by the settings in the backup.
Flic gestures are restored, but the buttons and wheel have to be paired again on this phone. Their Bluetooth bonds live in Android, not in the app.
A backup with this name already exists in your folder.
No backups found in the sync folder
Resets all app settings to their defaults.
Your paired devices and saved backups are kept. This can\'t be undone.
Records a trip when the wheel is connected
Starts on movement, auto-stops after idle, restarts next ride
Reconnects to the last paired wheel automatically
What happens when you swipe or press back from the dashboard
The current text-to-speech voice doesn\'t match the selected language. Switch to a %1$s voice for announcements?
Adjusts wheel-reported speed; affects alarms, voice and the trip log
Used when Legal Mode is on; must be lower than normal tiltback
Connect your wheel to edit speed limits
It will not be shown again
Welcome to EUC Planet, a no-nonsense, open-source app for electric unicycles
You can enable or disable individual voice messages later in Settings → Voice and announcements.
Connect and disconnect your wheel here
Many wheels just need testers to be fully supported, usually an easy fix. Tap the version to open About and help out
Many buttons have extra actions, try long-pressing them
Explore the powerful custom alarm editor in Settings → %1$s
Explore the integrations: helmet HUDs, WearOS watches, Flic buttons, Garmin Connect IQ, and the rear-view radar
Keep the eyes on the road and away from this app while unicycling!
Back up your trips in Settings → %1$s. Check them in %2$s, or analyze the backups at eucviewer.ried.no.\n\nIn the same section you can join the public leaderboards at eucstats.ried.no
Your live speed. Change units in Settings → %1$s
Live battery, temp, voltage and more. Rearrange them in Settings → %1$s
Quick controls. Tweak them in the same place, under %2$s
Open the navigator to plan routes and get turn-by-turn guidance
Tap for live data view here, you can even calibrate your wheel speed based on GPS speed. Check the calibration later in Settings → %1$s
Record video with a live telemetry overlay. Create custom fancy overlays and export them to make your own videos with data from past trips
Shows Park or Drive. Tap it to set your %1$s
Drives engine RPM live from wheel speed and motor load
Skip the phone speaker. Engine plays only with wired or Bluetooth audio
Hold the Flic 2 button 6 seconds for pairing mode, then tap Scan
%1$s will be unpaired and its actions cleared. You can pair it again anytime.
Remap each output axis to a raw axis on the box
Get ready to move the device
Press Next and walk forward for a few seconds
Press Next and walk left without changing the device orientation
Press Next and slowly lower the device to the ground
Adds a coloured dot on the speed dial with the GPS reading
Use phone volume buttons as shortcuts (only when app is visible)
Hold the Varia button for 2 seconds until it pulses purple, then start a scan.
Thin radar lane in the dashboard gutters. One coloured dot per detected vehicle. Only visible on the dashboard.
Turn on Bluetooth to scan for wheels.
Turn on if your wheel does not appear
This will permanently delete all recorded trips and their data files. This cannot be undone.
Delete this trip and its data file?
No data to show. Import or Record a new one
No data recorded in this trip
Turn on or off the headlights based on sunset and sunrise
Boost music volume based on speed
Sun doesn\'t set at this latitude today, lights will stay OFF
Sun doesn\'t rise at this latitude today, lights will stay ON
Suspended due to user light toggle for this session. Reconnect the wheel or restart the app to resume
For each metric the most relevant alarm fires automatically. The order in the list doesn\'t change which one; sorting just keeps it tidy.
Set the wheel speed calibration to %1$+.1f %%?\n\nYou can change this later in Wheel parameters.
Minimum time between fires for this rule, even if the condition keeps matching
Once: one alert, then re-arms after the reading returns to the safe side.\nMany: re-alerts every cooldown while it stays triggered.
One alert when %1$s. Re-arms when %2$s, after the %3$d s cooldown.
Alerts about %1$s s before %2$s, from the recent trend.
Now: alerts the instant the threshold is reported.\nOthers: alert that many seconds before the trend is about to reach it.

I got tired of EUC apps that evolve too slowly, look dated, have draconian rules around new features, treat their communities as customers, and forget that EUC riders are at their best when they help each other. This one tries to be the opposite.
CAUTION: Improper use of Service Mode could result in loss of wheel functionality, serious injury, or death
Service Mode is intended for qualified EUC professionals to conduct diagnostics, repairs, calibrations, and protocol research
While active, the app records every BLE byte sent to and received from the wheel plus any test commands you fire. The log lives in memory until the app is closed.
Speed updated from wheel: %1$d km/h tiltback, %2$d km/h alarm
Before joining, please read what becomes public:
Your overall riding stats, like distance and speed
Locations are clustered into wide areas of the map for privacy
Raw GPS tracks are never published.
Registration failed. Check your connection and try again.
This phone\'s leaderboard profile no longer exists on the server, so new uploads will be rejected. If this is unexpected, contact the leaderboard admin.
Send any trips not yet uploaded to the leaderboard.
A different rider\'s recovery id is already saved in this folder, kept it, didn\'t overwrite.
Online upload failed, tap to retry
Join to send your stats and trips to the public leaderboard at
Your stats and trips appear on the public leaderboard at
This phone stops uploading rides. Your rider ID stays saved so you can rejoin as the same rider. Your stats stay on EUC Stats; use Delete account to remove them.
You can change this again on %1$s
This permanently deletes your EUC Stats profile, stats, and uploaded data. This cannot be undone.
Held for a quick automated check. It joins the leaderboard once it clears.
We found a saved profile here: %1$s. Continuing rejoins the public leaderboard as that rider, its name, flag, avatar and ride stats are public. It does not create a new profile.
We found a different saved profile: %1$s. Continuing switches this phone to that rider; your current profile is backed up first.
The backup folder contains a profile that was used for the leaderboard.\n\nRider ID: %1$s
Camera access is needed for the viewports
Set a backup folder first to save presets
No saved presets yet. Save your current layout to add one.
The replay clip will not be saved.
This clears every overlay and resets the screen to a single camera viewport. Saved presets are not affected.
Loading \"%1$s\" replaces your current preset. Save the current one first if you want to keep it.
Presets are saved as individual .json files in your backup folder, under overlays/
Set up a backup folder to load and save presets
A live value like speed or battery
A rolling graph of a value
A circular dial gauge for a value
A linear bar gauge for a value
Your own image, embedded in the preset
A clock, analog watch or stopwatch
A live mini-map with your trace
No cameras detected on this device.
Android limits the number of camera streams, so with two cameras in use the rest are disabled.
Variables: {speed} {battery} {temp} {voltage} {current} {power} {pwm} {trip} {odometer} {pitch} {roll} {wheel}. Press enter for a new line.
Set your photo in Navigator → Customize my marker
The radar sees behind you but not which side, so both markers light by threat.
%1$s can\'t run alongside another camera on this device
Tap the wrench to choose an image
Up to a destination and 24 stops
Your current stops will be cleared.
Replace the current route with the loaded GPX?
Your route already has stops. Add this as the next stop, or start fresh?
Trips can also be opened on %1$s when linked.
Verify your Dropbox email address, then try sharing again
Couldn\'t link Dropbox. Check your connection and try again
Upload to your Dropbox folder and share the public link
Share a eucviewer.ried.no link to view this ride online
Copy a shareable eucviewer.ried.no link to the clipboard
Link Dropbox in settings to enable this
In the Stops list, long-press a stop to set it to your location
Tap the map, search, or load a GPX file
Routing service unavailable, using straight lines
Can\'t add a stop while navigation is running
Could not read that GPX file
Stop guidance for the active route?
You have arrived at your destination
How close counts as reaching a stop
How far off the route before the off-route alert triggers
Show chargers and places along the route
Off solves only the next stop and shows the rest as a dashed preview
Avoidances use a different routing service and apply where the travel mode supports them
Couldn\'t load places. Check your connection
Tip: hold this button to choose which places to show
Reviews & photos via Open Charge Map (CC BY)
Free public OpenStreetMap by default; change to use your own server
Adds reviews, photos and connector details to the charger flyout. Sign up for a free key at
Mirror the turn arrow and distance on the watch while navigating
Send the trip CSV to another app
Open it on the EUC Viewer map
Scrub the ride with live data overlays
Keep your phone on and this screen open while the render finishes
Non-transparent formats use the chroma key colour; may look off on semi-transparent layers
Power on the device and tap Pair to feed its speed and G-Force live, also saved to each trip CSV
Prefer the connected device over the phone for speed, position and G-Force
You won\'t see ride status in the status bar or get voice alerts on the notification shade
